findings.title: media.ts queries non-existent field planType on businessPages
findings.issue: Code queries `businessPages.planType` but this field does not exist in the schema. The subscriptions table handles plan/limits, not businessPages.
findings.evidence: Line 57: `const [business] = await db.select({ planType: businessPages.planType })` - planType field doesn't exist in businessPages schema definition.
findings.suggestion: Remove planType reference and use subscriptions table to determine plan limits, or remove the getBusinessPlanLimits helper if not used.
findings.impact: SQL error will occur when trying to query businessPages.planType

findings.title: media.ts sets wrong field for type during upload
findings.issue: Code sets `type: isImage ? 'image' : 'video'` but schema defines `type` as enum 'profile'|'banner'|'gallery'|'product'|'blog'. Should set `mediaType` instead.
findings.evidence: Line 268 and 377: `type: isImage ? 'image' : 'video'` - media.type uses category enum, not image/video. Should use media.mediaType field.
findings.suggestion: Change to: `mediaType: isImage ? 'image' : 'video'` on lines 268 and 377
findings.impact: Database will reject uploads because 'image'/'video' is not a valid value for the type enum

findings.title: Seed data price format incompatible with schema
findings.issue: Seed products use string prices ('$15.00', '$4.00') but schema defines price as integer (cents). Seed will either fail or store wrong values.
findings.evidence: Line 206: `{ price: '$15.00', ... }` - Schema line 110: `price: integer(price)` (expects cents)
findings.suggestion: Update seed products to use integer cents: 1500 for $15.00, 400 for $4.00, etc.
findings.impact: Seed data will have incorrect price values or insertion may fail

findings.title: Seed data still uses planType on orders table
findings.issue: Seed sampleOrders still insert planType field which is now deprecated. The subscriptions table should be used instead.
findings.evidence: Line 228: `{ planType: 'pro', amount: 5000, ... }` - orders schema uses planType legacy field
findings.suggestion: Update seed to use subscriptions table instead of orders, and remove planType from orders seed
findings.impact: Seed data follows deprecated schema structure

findings.title: Admin API endpoints lack server-side role verification
findings.issue: Admin API endpoints (GET, POST) have no server-side authentication or role verification. Client-side AdminLayout guard can be bypassed.
findings.evidence: users/index.ts lines 8-59 - no auth.session check, no role verification. Any request can list/create users.
findings.suggestion: Add server-side auth check: verify session token and ensure user.role is 'admin' or 'super_admin' before processing
findings.impact: Any authenticated user can access admin API endpoints by directly calling them

testResults.coverageNote: Build passes, tests pass. Critical issues are schema/data mismatch and security gaps.
